@graph.name: Land Rover Range Rover Velar D240 Engine Era Map
Land Rover Range Rover Velar D240 Engine Codes

mainEntity.name: How much does a Land Rover Range Rover Velar D240 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned 204DTD engine typically costs £3,500–£5,800 supply only, with used units available from £2,000–£3,500. Fitting adds £1,700–£2,500 depending on the workshop. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your Velar.

mainEntity.name: Is the Land Rover Range Rover Velar D240 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on the year. Pre-2019 cars are high-risk — the timing chain tensioner design flaw is well-documented. Post-2019 cars received revised tensioners and are significantly more robust. Both generations require diligent maintenance, particularly on oil dilution and DPF management.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Land Rover Range Rover Velar D240?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain tensioner wear (pre-2019), oil dilution from DPF regeneration, and EGR cooler leaks. The timing chain is the single most critical issue — address it preventatively or risk a full engine replacement.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Velar D240 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Velar values remain strong — a well-kept L560 is often worth £15,000–£35,000+. A £3,500–£5,800 reconditioned engine is only 15–30% of the vehicle's value — well within the Land Rover 60% scrap threshold. Replacement is almost always worthwhile on a cherished example.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Land Rover Range Rover Velar D240 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The engine code is 204DTD — it's the only 2.0-litre diesel used in the Velar D240. You can find it on the VIN plate, in the vehicle documentation, or on a label on the engine itself. Production years were 2017–2020.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the 204DTD timing chain tensioner, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: It needs replacement — the tensioners wear and the chain can stretch. A full timing chain, tensioner and guide replacement costs £1,200–£2,500. It's a labour-intensive job but far cheaper than a full engine replacement (£3,500+). The revised post-2019 components are the correct fix. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately.

replacementCosts.h2: Land Rover Range Rover Velar D240 Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the current Velar D240 market. This badge uses one engine across a short production run — no era ambiguity. Post-2019 engines with revised tensioners typically command a premium.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a Velar D240 engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer).

cta.label: → Compare D240 engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Timing Chain Tensioner Failure (Pre-2019)
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise on cold start lasting 2–5 seconds, metallic ticking from the front of the engine, engine warning light, cam timing errors, eventual chain slip
problems.replacementCost: £3,500–£5,800 (if chain jumps and causes valve interference)

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: any cold-start rattle on a pre-2019 Ingenium diesel requires immediate investigation

problems.recommendation: This is the single most critical issue on the early Ingenium diesel. The timing chain tensioner design was revised in 2019, but pre-2019 examples are at significant risk. If you hear cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately. A preventative chain and tensioner replacement is far cheaper than a full engine replacement. When buying, look for post-2019 build or evidence of timing chain work.

failureLink.label: Read the full timing chain failure guide

problems.title: Oil Dilution (Diesel in Oil)
problems.symptoms: Rising oil level on the dipstick, smell of diesel in the oil, engine warning light, DPF regeneration occurring frequently
problems.typicalMileage: Any mileage — exacerbated by short journeys
problems.repairCost: £150–£300 (oil and filter change with correct spec oil)
problems.recommendation: Diesel Ingenium engines suffer from oil dilution — fuel gets past the piston rings during DPF regeneration cycles. Short journeys make this worse. Check the oil level regularly; if it's rising, get an oil change immediately using the correct JLR-spec oil. Extended dilution damages bearings and leads to catastrophic failure. Regular motorway runs to allow passive DPF regeneration are essential.

failureLink.label: Read the full oil dilution guide

problems.title: EGR Cooler Leaks
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss with no visible external leak, white smoke from exhaust, occasional burning smell, engine warning light
problems.recommendation: The EGR cooler is a known weak point on the 204DTD. Coolant leaks from the cooler can lead to overheating if not caught early. Replacement is straightforward and parts are readily available from JLR specialists. Check coolant levels regularly and investigate any unexplained drop. If left unchecked, coolant loss can lead to head gasket failure.

failureLink.label: Read the full EGR cooler failure guide

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Velar D240 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: The most important upgrade — far cheaper than a full engine
If chain jumps and valves hit pistons, replacement is the only option
Oil change with correct spec — cheap and effective
Viable on a low‑mileage example; borderline on high‑mileage

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Range Rover Velar D240? Check These First
buyIf: * Post‑2019 build date — revised timing chain tensioners make this a significantly safer buy
* Pre‑2019 with documented timing chain work — evidence of the upgrade is essential
* Full service history with correct JLR‑spec oil (0W‑20 or 5W‑30)
* Evidence of regular motorway driving — DPF regeneration requires motorway runs
avoidIf: * Pre‑2019 with no proof of timing chain work — you're buying a known risk
* Any cold‑start rattle — this is almost certainly timing chain tensioner wear
* Evidence of rising oil levels — oil dilution is damaging bearings
* Poor service history — the Ingenium diesel is unforgiving of neglect

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 204DTD is a refined and efficient diesel that makes the Velar a genuinely compelling proposition. But pre‑2019 examples are high‑risk due to the timing chain tensioner design flaw. Post‑2019 cars are significantly improved. On any car, regular maintenance and motorway driving are essential to avoid DPF and oil dilution issues. Buy on history, maintain on schedule, and this engine will serve you well.

cta.label: → Compare D240 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Range Rover Velar D240 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: 1. Cold Start — Listen for rattles lasting more than 1–2 seconds (timing chain tensioner warning) — this is the single most critical check on the D240
2. Service History — including which generation (pre‑2019 or post‑2019) and evidence of timing chain work on pre‑2019 cars
3. Oil Level — Is the oil level rising on the dipstick? (oil dilution warning)
4. Coolant Level — Any unexplained loss? (EGR cooler warning sign)
5. DPF History — Check for regeneration issues; short journeys accelerate DPF wear
6. Oil Condition — Any signs of diesel contamination? (fuel in oil)
7. Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
8. MOT History — Check for emissions‑related failures (DPF, EGR, NOx sensor issues)

engineCodes.matchNote: This table's rows match Section 1B's Era Map exactly — one engine throughout this badge's production run.
